board.title: Ideas from Notable Computer Papers
board.author: Julius Boateng
board.description: Many ideas that began as research papers and shaped computer science are now accepted as common knowledge. This puzzle covers notable research papers that proposed ideas so foundational they are now taken for granted.

clues.question: Consensus protocol using majority agreement; described in Lamport's 1998 paper "The Part-Time Parliament"
Processor architecture using simple instructions; introduced in Patterson and Sequin's 1981 paper "___ I: A Reduced Instruction Set VLSI Computer"
Neural network architecture introduced in Google's landmark 2017 paper "Attention Is All You Need"; foundation of modern large language models
Public-key asymmetric encryption algorithm; introduced in Rivest, Shamir, and Adleman's 1978 paper
Probabilistic data structure for testing set membership; from Bloom's 1970 paper "Space/Time Trade-offs in Hash Coding with Allowable Errors"
Logical clock for ordering events; from Lamport's 1978 paper "Time, Clocks, and the Ordering of Events in a Distributed System"
Quick divide-and-conquer sorting algorithm that partitions an array around a pivot; introduced in Tony Hoare's 1961 eponymous paper
Key exchange algorithm for establishing a shared secret over a public channel; from Diffie and Hellman's 1976 paper "New Directions in Cryptography"
___ model; concurrency paradigm where actors communicate through messages; introduced in Hewitt, Bishop, and Steiger's 1973 paper
Page ranking algorithm used by Google search; introduced in Page and Brin's 1998 paper "The Anatomy of a Large-Scale Hypertextual Web Search Engine"
___ model; introduced in Codd's seminal 1970 paper "A Relational Model of Data for Large Shared Data Banks"
Classic consensus problem involving faulty participants; introduced in Lamport, Shostak, and Pease's 1982 paper "The ___ Generals Problem"
Balanced search tree with log-time operations; described in Bayer and McCreight's 1970 paper "Organization and Maintenance of Large Ordered Indices"
Consensus protocol using an elected leader; described in Ongaro and Ousterhout's 2014 paper "In Search of an Understandable Consensus Algorithm"
Wired networking standard; introduced in Metcalfe and Boggs' 1976 paper "___: Distributed Packet Switching for Local Computer Networks"
